text: Welcome to this video presentation. We would like to demonstrate how additional length for allele pouch anal endostomosis can be obtained to make the pouch reach without tension. At the same time, we aim to demonstrate the potential value of fluorescence angiography in this setting to assert an adequate perfusion. The case we would like to present regards a twenty-three year old female patient diagnosed with ulcerative colitis in two thousand and thirteen. Due to biological refractory disease, she underwent a subtotal colectomy and ileostomy procedure in May, two thousand and twenty. After convalescence, she was planned for a modified two-stage pouch procedure.

One of the Achilles heels of pouch surgery is anastomotic leakage. Insufficient reach of the pouch is a risk factor for developing anastomotic leakage due to traction on the anastomosis. For this reason, routine intraoperative lengthening measures are performed. These routine measures involve mobilization of the mesenteric root of the small bowel up to the duodenum and transverse peritoneal incisions approximately every three centimeters. This flowchart represents the pathway of lengthening measures. The yardstick of sufficient reach of the pouch is two centimeters below the lower border of the pubic bone.

The terminal ileum is exteriorized through the old ileostomy site, with white arrows indicating traction caused by interconnecting terminal ileal branches. Translumination reveals the vascular arcade within the terminal ileal mesentery. Macroscopic perfusion of the terminal ileum is assessed by clamping the interconnecting terminal ileal branches before ligation.

The surgeon ligates the arterial branches to obtain additional mesenteric length for the pouch, ensuring no perfusion anomalies during white light assessment. After pouch creation, a bolus of NDC green is injected, and fluorescence angiography reveals suboptimal perfusion at the pouch tip, which is reinforced with PDS-IV-Zero sutures.

The preoperative plan for a modified two-stage procedure was also changed into a three-stage procedure, as a protective ileostomy was created, even though there was now sufficient length. Endoluminal assessment of the pouch two weeks postoperative showed a well-vascularized and intact blind loop on the left image and anastomotic site on the right image. Thank you for your attention.
